title: Images and alt text
description: img is a void element with two required attributes.

questions.prompt: Which attribute describes an image for screen readers and broken-image fallback?
questions.explanation: alt is the accessible description. Never leave it out — even empty (alt="") is meaningful.

questions.prompt: For a PURELY DECORATIVE image (spacer, background flourish), the alt attribute should be:
questions.explanation: Empty alt="" tells assistive tech to skip. Omitting causes screen readers to read the filename.

questions.code: A) B) Logo C) src=logo.png D) alt="Logo"/>
questions.explanation: is a void element — no closing tag. src and alt as attributes.

questions.prompt: Which attributes help the browser reserve space and avoid layout shift?
questions.explanation: Explicit width/height (or aspect-ratio in CSS) prevent CLS on load.

options: Defers loading until the image is near the viewport
questions.explanation: Native lazy loading — one attribute, browser handles the rest.

questions.prompt: For responsive images at different sizes:
questions.explanation: srcset lists candidates; sizes hints the intended display width.

questions.prompt: Best format for photos with transparency at small size?
questions.explanation: PNG for lossless with alpha; WebP is smaller with the same feature set.
SVG is vector — sharp at any size, tiny file.
